title: The Forgotten City
shortDescription: An underground cavern holding the ruins of an ancient city of The Lost, with a shifting labyrinth and a central temple of sealed chambers.
atmosphere: A decaying civilization in perpetual abandonment: crumbling architecture, creeping vegetation and an oppressive sense of lost purpose.
tips: All technology fails — navigate by landmarks. Avoid entity-infested zones outside the temple. Map the shifting labyrinth and heed The Lost.

title: Eyes On You
shortDescription: A vast ancient cavern of valuable minerals, home to evolved Neanderthals and adapted entities, and a relentless feeling of being watched.
atmosphere: Cavernous darkness lit by distant torches; an overwhelming sense of surveillance builds mounting psychological pressure.
tips: Stay constantly aware of your surroundings. Keep almond water handy. Don't explore deep — the entities are passive but unpredictable.

title: Alone
shortDescription: A single house suspended in an eternal morning, where a trapped wanderer lives on mysteriously restocking food and faceless visitors.
atmosphere: An isolated home under perpetual dawn: peaceful yet profoundly lonely, comfort at war with inescapable confinement.
tips: Rescue is possible via outside contact — leave written messages where they can be found. The faceless visitors mean no immediate harm.

title: Stairwell Of Spirals
shortDescription: An infinite spiraling stairwell of decaying wooden steps and damp blue walls, threaded with mysterious hallways and rare entities.
atmosphere: Damp and moldy, water dripping down the walls; creaking stairs and distant ocean sounds deepen a pervasive loneliness.
tips: Avoid the water-filled rooms and stay alert for rare entities. Use the visible doors strategically to reach safer levels.

title: Pit Stop
shortDescription: An infinite abandoned restaurant complex used as a temporary refuge; wanderers are automatically displaced after random intervals.
atmosphere: Eerily quiet under perpetual sunlight; damp cool air around 21°C brings drowsiness and quiet introspection.
tips: Use it only as a brief sanctuary. Food and water are available. Explore carefully — the average stay is about three days.

title: Frostbite
shortDescription: A circular ice-cavern system of ever-colder zones, reaching absolute zero at a deadly frozen core.
atmosphere: A desolate frozen void lit by bioluminescent ice; temperature extremes make survival impossible without specialized gear.
tips: Find supply crates with cold suits and oxygen. Follow red flags toward outposts. Avoid ice burrows and never touch the singularity floor.

title: Diurnal Art Gallery
shortDescription: A welcoming art gallery of surreal paintings depicting Backrooms levels, kept by a friendly teenage artist and a veiled figure.
atmosphere: A warm creative sanctuary of shifting layouts, paint and wood scents, and soft light that links you to the levels on the walls.
tips: Ask The Painter for directions. Avoid the unnamed painting at the back of the Registry. Approaching a painting immerses you in its level.

title: Water Wonder
shortDescription: An abandoned indoor waterpark of pools, corridors, cafes and storerooms, home to the Tribe of Tegan and a B.N.T.G. base.
atmosphere: Decaying amusement facilities where a primitive tribal settlement and an organized corporate outpost coexist in eerie duality.
tips: Avoid entities in the corridors. Befriend the Tribe of Tegan for resources and harvest ripe Tegan Fruit for food and protective skin.

title: (page unavailable)
shortDescription: Source page returned HTTP 404 at fetch time; kept as a stub so cross-references resolve. Data to be filled on a re-fetch.

title: The Baywalk
shortDescription: A dark bayside concrete promenade with broken streetlights, prowled by Smilers and a calming mental effect that lures wanderers to drown.
atmosphere: An unsettling seaside walk of malfunctioning lights and constant Smiler presence, laced with an insidious 'painless death' pull.
tips: Travel in groups to resist the Painless Death effect. Avoid the upper area; prep in the lower dirt section. Remember the Guide.
